Title: Test user authentication
Preconditions: User has a valid email and password
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application
2. Navigate to the login page
3. Enter the email and password
4. Click the login button
Expected Result: The user is successfully logged in and redirected to the main page

Title: Test user registration
Preconditions: User does not have an account
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application
2. Navigate to the registration page
3. Enter the email and password
4. Click the register button
Expected Result: A new user account is created, and the user is logged in and redirected to the main page

Title: Test creating a report
Test Data:
- Title: Test Report
- Description: This is a test report
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a user
2. Navigate to the report creation page
3. Enter the title and description
4. Click the create report button
Expected Result: A new report is created and displayed on the user's report list

Title: Test modifying a report
Preconditions: User is logged in and has a created report
Test Data:
- New Title: Updated Test Report
- New Description: This is an updated test report
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a user
2. Navigate to the report list
3. Select a created report
4. Click the edit button
5. Enter the new title and description
6. Click the update report button
Expected Result: The report is updated with the new title and description

Title: Test creating a calendar entry
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a user
2. Navigate to the calendar creation page
3. Enter the calendar name
4. Click the create calendar button
Expected Result: A new calendar is created and displayed on the user's calendar list

Title: Test retrieving calendar entries
Preconditions: User is logged in and has created calendars
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a user
2. Navigate to the calendar list
3. Select a created calendar
4. Click the view button

Title: Test retrieving train information
Preconditions: User is logged in and has a selected calendar
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a user
2. Navigate to the selected calendar
3. Click the view trains button
Expected Result: The train information for the selected calendar is displayed

Title: Test setting a preferred train
Preconditions: User is logged in and has a selected calendar with train information
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a user
2. Navigate to the selected calendar
3. Click the set preferred train button for a specific train
Expected Result: The selected train is set as the preferred train for the calendar

Title: Test deleting a preferred train
Preconditions: User is logged in and has a selected calendar with a preferred train
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a user
2. Navigate to the selected calendar
3. Click the delete preferred train button for the preferred train
Expected Result: The preferred train is deleted from the calendar

Title: Test displaying train variations
Preconditions: User is logged in and has a selected calendar with train information
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a user
2. Navigate to the selected calendar
3. Click the show notifications button
Expected Result: The train variations are displayed as notifications

Title: Test system notifications
Preconditions: User is logged in and has created reports
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a user
2. Create a report
3. Modify the report
4. Delete the report
Expected Result: The system sends notifications for each report action (creation, modification, deletion) to the user and the database

Title: Test system error handling
Preconditions: User is logged in and has created reports
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a user
2. Create a report with invalid data (e.g., empty title or description)
Expected Result: The system handles the error and does not create the report, displaying an error message to the user

Title: Test system performance under load
Preconditions: User is logged in and has created multiple reports
Test Steps:
1. Log in as a user
2. Create a large number of reports (e.g., 1000 reports) within a short period
Expected Result: The system handles the load and creates all reports without crashing or displaying errors, while maintaining acceptable performance levels.
